task: I’m working on a school robotics thing and need to write a short intro for my part. The group is keeping the final presentation brief, so I want something that clearly says what I handled without turning it into a whole speech.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a short, clean intro you can use:

“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I worked on [your part], mainly helping with [specific task]. I also helped keep things organized so we could get everything ready for the final presentation.”

If you want, I can make it:
- more casual
- more confident
- more specific to your exact role

user_simulator.text: Too generic. I need it to sound like I actually did something real, not just “helped with” stuff. Make it shorter and more specific to robotics/coding, like I handled the control code or testing or whatever. And keep it confident, not cheesy.

turns.agent_response:
Got it — here are a few shorter, more specific options:

1. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I handled the control code and tested it on the robot.”**

2. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I worked on the robot’s coding and made sure it ran correctly.”**

3. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I wrote and tested the code for the robot’s controls.”**

4. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I was in charge of the control code and debugging.”**

If you want, I can make it sound:
- more technical
- more student-like
- more impressive without sounding fake

user_simulator.text: Closer. 1 and 4 are basically the right vibe, but still a little too clean. I want it to sound more like I actually owned the code part, not just stood near it. Give me 3-4 versions that are even shorter and a little sharper — like I wrote the control sequence, debugged it, and got the robot moving right. No cheesy “I was in charge of” stuff.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are 4 shorter, sharper versions:

1. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I wrote the control code, debugged it, and got the robot moving right.”**

2. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I handled the control code and fixed it until the robot ran properly.”**

3. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I wrote the robot controls, debugged them, and tested the movement.”**

4. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I built the control sequence, debugged it, and got it working on the robot.”**

If you want, I can make them even:
- more casual
- more technical
- more confident/competitive

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s basically it. 1 and 4 work.
